home.tagline: A caring platform for children with ADHD and their parents. Together, we move forward, one small step at a time.
home.explorerDesc: For children. Games, quests and stars to progress every day!
home.parentsDesc: For parents. Tools, advice and strategies to support with serenity.

profile.ageHint: Displayed games automatically adapt to age

thermostat.title: 🌡️ How are you feeling right now?
thermostat.subtitle: Choose, the app helps you find the right activity.

calme.label: Calm
calme.message: Your brain is at its best! It's the ideal time for cognitive challenges.
calme.science: A calm state activates the prefrontal cortex — your concentration center.

energique.label: Energetic
energique.message: Great energy! Direct it first with a movement game, then move on to challenges.
energique.science: Physical exercise releases dopamine and improves concentration for 30 min.

stresse.label: Overwhelmed
stresse.message: Your brain is overloaded. Start by breathing — you'll manage better after.
stresse.science: Slow breathing activates the vagus nerve and reduces cortisol in 90 seconds.

triste.label: Sad
triste.message: It's normal to feel that way. Take care of yourself — a gentle activity will help.
triste.science: Creative expression helps process difficult emotions and name them.

agite.label: Restless
agite.message: Too much energy inside! Move to release it, then your brain will be freer.
agite.science: ADHD restlessness is often a sign of dopamine under-stimulation — movement helps.

explorer.alertNoAgeDesc: Ask a parent to set your age in your profile to see adapted games.
explorer.superDesc: Discover what makes your brain special ✨

sections.attentionDesc: Stay focused even when it's long
sections.memoryDesc: Keep information in your head while you work
sections.organisationDesc: Break big tasks down, order the steps
sections.emotionsDesc: Recognize, name and soothe your emotions
sections.readingDesc: Strengthen reading, spelling and phonological processing

signal-stop.name: Stop Signal
signal-stop.desc: Stop only when you need to

sabr.desc: Let the plant grow without touching

stroop.desc: Read the ink color, not the word

intrus.desc: Find the emoji that doesn't fit!

vigilance.name: Vigilance ⭐
vigilance.desc: Tap only the star in the flow

chrono.name: Magic Chrono
chrono.desc: Stop the timer at the right moment

nujum.name: Sky Map
nujum.desc: Trace the path through the stars

mot-mystere.name: Mystery Word
mot-mystere.desc: Memorize the word, retype its letters

mizan.name: The Great Scale
mizan.desc: Sort each card to the right side

mission-ordre.name: Order Mission
mission-ordre.desc: Put the daily steps in order

quete.name: 3-Step Quest
quete.desc: Break your big challenge into simple steps

emotions.name: Emotion Detective
emotions.desc: Identify the emotion in the situation

syllabes.name: Syllable Tap
syllabes.desc: Tap the syllables of the word

creatif.name: Creative Challenge
creatif.desc: Roll the dice, create a story

parents.heroSubtitle: Tools, advice and strategies to move forward together.

brain.title: Understanding the ADHD brain
brain.preview: Dopamine, executive functions, inhibition… What's really happening.

communication.title: Talking with kindness
communication.preview: Key phrases, tone of voice, active listening to avoid conflicts.

crisis.title: Crisis in 3 steps
crisis.preview: Your child is overwhelmed. Here's the immediate, calm action plan.

routine.title: Why routine protects
routine.preview: Predictability reduces anxiety and frees up mental bandwidth.

rewards.title: Rewards that work
rewards.preview: Token economy, 5:1 ratio, immediate timing — the validated method.

reading.title: Reading & writing with ADHD
reading.preview: Orton-Gillingham, two-color method, short sessions — ADHD pros.

quest.title: Quest in 3 Micro-Steps
quest.goalPlaceholder: E.g.: Tidy my room, Do my homework…
quest.bravoDesc: You completed your quest! +5 stars earned ✨

powers.pageSubtitle: Your ADHD brain is different — not inferior. Here's what makes it special.
powers.closingTitle: You don't have a broken brain.
powers.closingDesc: You have a different brain — and difference, once understood, becomes a true strength.

hyperfocus.tagline: You can go further than anyone
hyperfocus.description: When something truly fascinates you, you can concentrate with incredible intensity — far more than most people. It's a rare superpower.
hyperfocus.science: Hyperfocus is linked to dopamine — your brain surges when it finds something fascinating.

creativite.tagline: Your brain sees connections others miss
creativite.description: Your brain makes unexpected links between very different ideas. That's creative thinking — and the greatest inventors, artists and entrepreneurs have had it.
creativite.science: Divergent thinking is significantly more developed in ADHD brains.

energie.description: This energy you sometimes don't know what to do with — it's fuel. People with ADHD who learn to direct it can accomplish extraordinary things.
energie.science: ADHD energy comes from high sensitivity to the reward system — it becomes a strength when well directed.

empathie.tagline: You feel others' emotions very deeply
empathie.description: Many children with ADHD feel others' emotions very intensely. It's not a weakness — it's a rare sensitivity that can become a great strength.
empathie.science: Heightened emotional sensitivity is linked to the same limbic system hyper-reactivity that makes ADHD intense.

resilience.tagline: You've already overcome more than you think
resilience.description: Every time you held on despite difficulties — you built something invisible but very solid within you.
resilience.science: Adults with ADHD who succeed often describe the resilience built in childhood as their greatest strength.

spontaneite.description: You're often the first to try something new, to pitch a wild idea, to act without overthinking. In many situations, that's exactly what's needed.
spontaneite.science: Controlled risk-taking and spontaneity are valued traits in entrepreneurs, artists and leaders.

signal-stop.subtitle: Cognitive training — Inhibition
signal-stop.tip: This game trains your inner brake — the ability to stop even when you want to click. It's a super skill!

sabr.subtitle: Inhibition × Delayed Gratification — Barkley 2011
sabr.tip: Let the plant grow without touching it. Patience is a real cognitive muscle!

stroop.subtitle: Stroop Effect × Cognitive Inhibition
stroop.tip: Read the ink color, not the word! Your brain must brake its automatic response.

minuteur.subtitle: Focus & Perseverance
minuteur.tip: Choose a duration, a mission, and stay focused to the end. Each completed session = stars earned!

intrus.subtitle: Selective Attention — Visual Discrimination · 6–10 yrs
intrus.tip: This game trains your selective attention — the ability to quickly spot what's different!

vigilance.subtitle: Sustained Attention × CPT — core ADHD deficit
vigilance.tip: Tap only the target when it appears. Vigilance is one of the keys to academic success!

chrono.subtitle: Time Perception × Inhibition — Barkley 2011
chrono.tip: Stop the timer as close to the target as possible. ADHD affects time perception — train it!

memoire.subtitle: Training · Working Memory
memoire.tip: Watch the emoji sequence, then recall it in the right order. The further you go, the longer the sequence!

paires.subtitle: Memory Game — Concentration · 6–8 yrs
paires.tip: Flip the cards and find the pairs — train your visual memory and concentration!

chiffres.subtitle: Number Memory — Digit Span · 8–12 yrs
chiffres.tip: Digits appear one by one then disappear — remember the sequence and tap it in order!

grille.subtitle: Spatial Memory — Concentration · 9–13 yrs
grille.tip: A colored grid appears briefly — memorize the cells and reproduce them!

dhikr.subtitle: Working Memory × Phonological Loop — Bhide 2013
dhikr.tip: Watch the light sequence, then reproduce it in the same order!

nujum.subtitle: Spatial Memory × Hippocampus — Martinussen 2005
nujum.tip: Watch the path light up in the starry sky, then retrace it by tapping the stars!

mot-mystere.subtitle: Visual Memory × Spelling
mot-mystere.tip: Memorize the word, then retype its letters in the right order!

rythme.subtitle: Simon Says — Memory & Rhythm · 9–12 yrs
rythme.tip: Watch the color sequence light up — then reproduce it in the same order!

tri-eclair.subtitle: Cognitive Flexibility · Processing Speed
tri-eclair.tip: An emoji appears: sort it into the right category as fast as possible!

mizan.subtitle: Cognitive Flexibility × Set-Shifting
mizan.tip: Sort each card to the right side of the scale. The category changes each level!

contraires.subtitle: Processing Speed × Cognitive Flexibility
contraires.tip: You see a word — choose its opposite as fast as possible! 60 seconds to score as many points as you can.

mission-ordre.subtitle: Planning × Sequencing — Executive Functions
mission-ordre.tip: Put the daily steps in the right order — train your planning skills!

puzzle.subtitle: Planning · Working Memory · 7–12 yrs
puzzle.tip: Slide the pieces to put the numbers in order. Train multi-step planning!

quete.subtitle: Organization & Micro-Goals
quete.tip: Break your big challenge into 3 small steps — every little step counts!

bouge.subtitle: Body Movement — Coordination · 4–8 yrs
bouge.tip: Movement activates the brain! Follow the physical challenges to improve your coordination and concentration.

emotions.subtitle: Emotional Intelligence × Empathy
emotions.tip: Identify the emotion in each situation — train your emotional intelligence!

scan-relax.subtitle: Progressive Muscle Relaxation × Emotional Regulation
scan-relax.tip: Release each part of your body following the guide. Physical relaxation soothes the mind!

bulle.subtitle: Guided Breathing · Cardiac Coherence
bulle.tip: Guided breathing calms the nervous system in minutes. Follow the bubble with your eyes and breathe at its pace.

dictee.subtitle: Spelling Memory · 7–12 yrs
dictee.tip: Memorize the word in 3 seconds, then reconstruct it with the scrambled letters!

syllabes.subtitle: Phonological Awareness · 5–8 yrs
syllabes.tip: Syllable awareness is the #1 predictor of reading. Tap-tap-tap the syllables!

couleurs.subtitle: Visual Activity — Color Recognition · 3–5 yrs
couleurs.tip: This game trains visual recognition and attention — find the animal's color as fast as possible!

creatif.subtitle: Free Writing — Creativity · 13+ yrs
creatif.tip: A random topic, a timer, and you let your imagination speak! Train your creative expression.

lecture.subtitle: Two-Color Method — Syllabic Decoding · 6–10 yrs
lecture.tip: Syllables alternate in blue and red to help your brain break words apart. Read aloud!

signal-stop.idle: Press START and click when it's 🟢 green — STOP if red!

sabr.idle: Let the plant grow without touching it. The longer you wait, the more it blooms. You can harvest whenever you want — but patience pays off!
sabr.science: 🔬 Trains the prefrontal cortex — the brain's self-control center
sabr.winScience: 🔬 By waiting, you strengthened your prefrontal cortex — the seat of self-control.
tips: Watch your seed… it needs time 🌱
Every second of waiting makes it grow 🌿
Feeling the urge to touch? Hold on… 💪
Your prefrontal cortex gets stronger every second!
Last round — show all your patience 🌻

memoire.idle: Memorize the emoji sequence, then reproduce it in the right order!
memoire.motivation: 🌟 You trained your working memory. Every game makes you stronger!

rythme.idle: Watch the color sequence light up — then reproduce it in the same order!
rythme.science: 🧠 This game trains your working memory and sense of rhythm!

tri-eclair.idle: An emoji appears — sort it into the right category as fast as possible! You have {time} seconds.
tri-eclair.science: 💡 This game trains your cognitive flexibility — the ability to switch ideas quickly. Superstar!

dhikr.idle: Watch the light sequence, then reproduce it in the same order! Difficulty increases each round.
dhikr.science: 🔬 Trains working memory — a core ADHD deficit (Martinussen 2005)
dhikr.loseDesc: Round {round} — every attempt strengthens the brain!
dhikr.winScience: 🔬 You just trained your phonological loop and visuospatial sketchpad.

nujum.idle: Watch the path light up in the starry sky, then retrace it in the right order!
nujum.science: 🔬 Trains spatial memory — impaired in ADHD (Martinussen 2005)
nujum.loseDesc: Level {level} — every attempt strengthens your spatial memory!
nujum.winScience: 🔬 Your hippocampus and parietal cortex just got a workout!

mizan.idle: Sort each card to the right side of the scale! Train your mental flexibility by changing category each level.
mizan.science: 🔬 Trains cognitive flexibility — the ADHD brain learns to switch rules (set-shifting)
mizan.winScience: 🔬 Your brain just practiced rule-switching — a key prefrontal cortex function.

contraires.idle: You see a word — choose its opposite as fast as possible! 60 seconds to score as many points as you can.
contraires.science: 🔬 Trains processing speed and mental flexibility
contraires.winScience: 🔬 Processing speed improves with regular practice.

minuteur.pauseDesc: 10 min of movement = 30 min more concentration. Choose an activity before restarting:
minuteur.actWaterDesc: Look out the window for 2 min
